item.ptxt: A/T consists of highly precise parts. As even flaw in small part may cause oil leakage or decrease in function, check each part carefully before installation.
Clean all parts with compressed air. Never use wiping cloths or rags.
Before assembling new clutch or brake discs, soak them in ATF for at least 2 hours.
Lubricate O-rings with ATF.
Apply ATF on sliding or rotating surfaces of parts before assembly.
Use SUZUKI Super Grease C to retain parts in place.
Be sure to install thrust bearings and races in correct direction and position.
Make sure that snap ring ends are not aligned with one of cut outs and are installed in groove correctly.
Do not use adhesive cements on gaskets and similar parts.
Be sure to torque each bolt and nut to specification.

s1.ptxt: Install new manual shift shaft oil seal to transaxle case evenly at specified position as shown in figure.
Use special tool and hammer it to install oil seal, then apply grease to its lip portion.

spec.title: Manual shift shaft oil seal installing depth

s1.ptxt: Install manual detent spring (1) to transaxle case and tighten manual detent spring bolt to specified torque.
Install parking lock pawl rod (2) to new manual valve lever (1).
After applying ATF to new manual valve lever (1), install new manual shift shaft (4), new spacer (3) and manual valve lever to transaxle case.

After installing new manual valve lever pin (2) using spring pin remover with 3 mm (0.1 in.) in diameter (5) and hammer, turn spacer to set position as shown in figure. Then caulk spacer with punch (6).
Install parking lock pawl bracket (1) to transaxle case by tightening its mounting bolt to specified torque.
After applying ATF to new O-rings, install them to 1st and reverse brake piston (3).
Install 1st and reverse brake piston (1) to transaxle case (2).

attention3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage O-ring when installing 1st and reverse brake piston.

s1.ptxt: After applying ATF to 1st and reverse brake return spring subassembly (1), install it to transaxle case (2).
Apply ATF to 1st and reverse brake discs (2), separator plates (3) and retaining plate (1), then install them to transaxle case.
Compress 1st and reverse brake return spring subassembly using special tool and hydraulic press, then attach new snap ring.

attention2.ptxt: Do not damage 1st and reverse brake return spring subassembly, discs, plates and piston by pressing in 1st and reverse brake return spring subassembly passing its original installing position more than 0.8 mm (0.03 in.).

s1.ptxt: Install new 1st and reverse brake snap ring so that its both ends would be positioned in correct location as shown in figure.
Measure 1st and reverse brake piston stroke.
If piston stroke exceeds specified value, disassemble, inspect and replace discs and plates.

item.ptxt: Using special tool, measure 1st and reverse brake piston stroke when compressed air (400 – 800 kPa, 4.1 – 8.2 kgf/cm2, 58.0 – 116 psi) is blown through oil hole.

s1.ptxt: Press-fit reduction drive gear (1) to transaxle case (3) using special tools and hydraulic press as shown in figure.

item.ptxt: Do not use transaxle case as groundwork to press fit reduction drive gear.
Do not give load more than 20 kN (2039.4 kgf, 4496.2 lbf) with hydraulic press. Otherwise, it may result in damaging reduction drive gear bearing.

attention3.ptxt: When replacing reduction drive gear, replace it together with reduction driven gear as a set.

entry.ptxt: Stand that can slightly lift transaxle case.

s1.ptxt: Install parking lock pawl (1) and spring (2). Apply ATF to parking lock pawl shaft, then insert it into transaxle case.
Press-fit new planetary ring gear subassembly (1) to reduction drive gear (3) using special tools and hydraulic press as shown in figure.

item.ptxt: Do not reuse planetary ring gear subassembly. Otherwise it may cause damage to planetary gear unit and/or reduction gears.
Do not use transaxle case as groundwork to press fit planetary ring gear subassembly.
Do not give load more than 20 kN (2039.4 kgf, 4496.2 lbf) with hydraulic press. Otherwise, it may result in damaging reduction drive gear bearing.

entry.ptxt: Stand that can slightly lift transaxle case.

s1.ptxt: Install new reduction drive gear nut.
Measure preload by turning counter drive gear at a speed of 100 rpm using a small torque wrench.
If measured preload is out of specification preload, replace following parts.

item.ptxt: A/T case
Reduction drive gear
Planetary ring gear sub assembly
Reduction drive gear nut
Do not tighten nut over specified torque.
Carry out this procedure on rubber mat in order not to damage transaxle case.

spec.title: Reduction drive gear bearing preload measured as starting torque

s1.ptxt: Using chisel or the like, caulk reduction drive gear nut (1).
Apply ATF to planetary gear assembly (1), and fit it to planetary ring gear assembly.
Check for correct installation of planetary gear assembly as follows.
Measure distance “a” using vernier caliper (1) and straightedge (2). If check result is not as specification, remove planetary gear assembly and reinstall it properly.

spec.title: Distance between planetary gear assembly and mating surface of transaxle case

s1.ptxt: Apply ATF to one-way clutch No.2 assembly (1), and install it to planetary gear assembly. After that, ensure that planetary carrier rotates only in counterclockwise direction [a], not in clockwise direction [b].
Install one-way clutch outer race retainer (1).
Apply ATF to new brake drum gasket (1) and install it to transaxle case.
Apply ATF to 2nd brake retaining plate (1), discs (2) and separator plates (3), and install them to transaxle case.
Apply ATF to 2nd brake return spring subassembly (1), and install it to transaxle case.
After applying ATF to 2nd brake piston assembly (1), align projection of 2nd brake piston assembly with groove of transaxle case, then put them together.
Compress 2nd brake piston assembly using special tool and hydraulic press, and install new 2nd brake piston snap ring (1).

attention2.ptxt: Do not damage 2nd brake piston assembly, return spring subassembly, plates and discs by pressing in 2nd brake assembly passing its original installing position more than 0.4 mm (0.02 in.).

s1.ptxt: After applying ATF to front sun gear thrust bearing race (2), install it to front planetary sun gear (1) in direction shown in figure.
After applying ATF to front planetary sun gear (1), install it to planetary gear assembly (2).
Install new O/D and 2nd coast brake retaining plate snap ring (1).

attention2.ptxt: Be sure to install new O/D and 2nd coast brake retaining plate snap ring correctly in groove of transaxle case.

s1.ptxt: After applying grease to sliding contact face of planetary carrier thrust washer (1), install it to planetary gear assembly.
After applying ATF to one-way clutch No.1 assembly (3), install it to rear planetary sun gear subassembly (2).
After applying ATF to planetary gear thrust bearing (1), install it to one-way clutch No.1 assembly in direction shown in figure.
After applying ATF to rear planetary sun gear subassembly and one-way clutch No.1 assembly (1), install them in transaxle case (2).
After applying ATF to rear sun gear thrust bearing race (1), install it to rear planetary sun gear (2) in direction shown in figure.
After applying ATF to rear sun gear thrust bearing (1), install it to forward clutch hub (2) in direction shown in figure.
After applying ATF to forward clutch hub (1), install it in transaxle case (2).

After applying ATF to intermediate shaft thrust bearing rear race (3), thrust bearing (2) and front race (1), install them to forward and reverse clutch assembly (4) by referring to the following table.
Apply ATF to forward and reverse clutch assembly (1).
Install forward and reverse clutch assembly while rotating clockwise and counterclockwise frequently to fit clutch discs to mating hubs.

attention3.ptxt: Before installation, align teeth of forward and reverse clutch discs to facilitate installation.

s1.ptxt: Check for correct installation of forward and reverse clutch assembly as follows.

Measure distance “a” using vernier caliper (1) and straightedge (2). If check result is not as specification, remove forward and reverse clutch assembly, forward clutch hub, rear planetary sun gear subassembly and one-way clutch No.1 assembly, and reinstall them properly.

spec.title: Distance between forward and reverse clutch assembly and mating surface of transaxle case

s1.ptxt: After applying ATF to O/D and 2nd coast brake retaining plate (4), separator plate (3), discs (2) and rear plate (1), install them to transaxle case (5).
Measure O/D and 2nd coast brake piston stroke.

item.ptxt: Measure dimension “a” from end face of transaxle case to O/D and 2nd coast brake rear plate (1) using straightedge and vernier caliper.
Measure dimension “b” from O/D and 2nd coast brake piston (2) to rear cover assembly mating surface using straightedge and vernier caliper.
Calculate piston stroke from measured value of dimensions “a” and “b”.
Piston stroke = “a” – “b”

spec.title: O/D and 2nd coast brake piston stroke

s1.ptxt: If check result is not as specification, select O/D and 2nd coast brake rear plate with suitable thickness from among the following table and replace it.

spec.title: Available O/D and 2nd coast brake rear plate thickness

s1.ptxt: After applying ATF to new 2nd brake gaskets (1), install them to transaxle case (2).
After applying ATF to reverse clutch drum thrust bearing (1), install it to forward and reverse clutch assembly (2) in direction shown in figure.
Remove sealant attached to mating surface of transaxle rear cover (1) completely.
Apply sealant to mating surface of transaxle rear cover (1) as shown in figure.
Install transaxle rear cover assembly (1) on transaxle case (2) and tighten rear cover bolts (3) to specified torque.
Install fluid reservoir left plate (1) by tightening its bolt to specified torque.
After applying ATF to new governor gasket No.2 (1), install it to transaxle case.
After applying ATF to differential assembly (1) and countershaft assembly (2), install them to transaxle case.
After applying ATF to direct clutch hub (1), install it to planetary gear assembly.
After applying ATF to input shaft rear thrust bearing (1) and thrust bearing race (2), install them into direct clutch hub (3) in direction shown in figure.
After applying ATF to input shaft front thrust bearing (1), install it to direct clutch assembly (2) in direction shown in figure.
After applying ATF to direct clutch assembly (1), install it while rotating clockwise and counterclockwise frequently to fit clutch discs to mating hub.

attention3.ptxt: Before installation, align teeth of direct clutch discs to facilitate installation.

s1.ptxt: Check for correct installation of direct clutch assembly as follows.
Measure distance “a” using vernier caliper (1) and straightedge (2). If check result is not as specification, remove direct clutch assembly, direct clutch hub and reinstall them properly.

spec.title: Distance between direct clutch assembly and mating surface of transaxle case

s1.ptxt: Install oil pump assembly (1) to transaxle case and tighten oil pump assembly bolts to specified torque.
Make sure that input shaft (1) turns smoothly.
Measure input shaft thrust play by applying dial gauge onto input shaft end (1).
If check result is not as specification, select input shaft front thrust bearing with suitable thickness from the following and replace it.

spec.title: Available input shaft front thrust bearing thickness

s1.ptxt: After applying ATF to new O-ring, fit it to breather union (2). Then install breather union to transaxle case.
Install breather hose (1).
Wipe off and clean mating surface between transaxle case (1) and torque converter housing.
Apply sealant to torque converter housing as shown in figure.
Install torque converter housing to transaxle case.

s2.ptxt: Apply sealant to threads of four bolts (1).

s1.ptxt: After applying ATF to new O-ring, fit it to fluid pressure check hole bolt (1). Then install fluid pressure check hole bolt to transaxle case (2) by tightening it to specified torque.
Install new O-rings to each accumulator piston and apply ATF to them referring to the following table.

attention3.ptxt: Make sure that O-rings are not twisted or caught when installing.

s1.ptxt: Install B1, C1, C2 accumulator pistons and springs referring to the following table.

entry.ptxt: Identification as embossed letters on piston

s1.ptxt: After applying ATF to cooler check valve (1) and spring (2), install them to transaxle case (3).
After applying ATF to new governor gasket No.1 (1), install it to transaxle case.
Install solenoid connector and valve body assembly.
Install A/T oil pan and oil strainer assembly.
After applying ATF to new O-rings (1), fit it to fluid cooler pipe unions (2). Then install fluid cooler pipe unions to transaxle case by tightening to specified torque.
Install fluid cooler pipe (4) and new gaskets (3) by tightening fluid cooler union bolts and fluid cooler pipe bracket bolt to specified torque.
After applying ATF to new O-ring (5), fit it to fluid filler tube (6). Then install fluid filler tube to transaxle case and tightening fluid filler tube bolt to specified torque.
After applying ATF to O-rings of each sensor, install input shaft speed sensor (1) and output shaft speed sensor (2) by tightening its mounting bolts to specified torque.
Install transmission range sensor.
Install hook (1) and tightening hook bolt to specified torque.
Install harness brackets and select cable clamp.
Install torque converter (3) noting the following points.

item.ptxt: Before installing converter, make sure that its pump hub portion is free from nicks, burrs or damage which may cause oil seal to leak.
Do not drop converter on oil pump gear. Damage in gear, may cause a critical trouble.
Install torque converter while aligning grooves (1) of torque converter with projection (2) of oil pump drive gear.
Install torque converter using care not to damage oil seal of oil pump.
After installing torque converter check that distance “a” is within specification.
Check torque converter for smooth rotation.
Apply grease around cup (6) at the center of torque converter.
